question.text: Which of the following tax changes was introduced by the Union Budget 2024 (effective from 23 July 2024) regarding mutual fund taxation?

answer.text: Introduction of DDT (Dividend Distribution Tax) on mutual fund dividends at 10%
Removal of Section 80C deduction for ELSS investments
STCG rate on equity MFs increased from 15% to 20% and LTCG rate increased from 10% to 12.5%, with exemption raised to Rs. 1.25 lakh
Restoration of indexation benefit for debt mutual funds purchased after April 2023

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** Budget 2024 (effective 23 July 2024) made these key changes: (1) Equity STCG rate increased from 15% to 20% under Section 111A; (2) Equity LTCG rate increased from 10% to 12.5% under Section 112A; (3) LTCG exemption threshold raised from Rs. 1,00,000 to Rs. 1,25,000 per year.
**The Trap:** Option A refers to DDT which was abolished in 2020, not reintroduced. Option B is false - ELSS continues under 80C. Option D is false - indexation was NOT restored for post-April 2023 debt funds.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Budget 2024 changes are highly examinable as they represent the latest amendments. The rates moved in opposite directions from what investors expected - rates increased but exemption also increased slightly.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** When discussing Budget 2024 changes with clients, emphasize that despite rate increases, the higher exemption of Rs. 1.25 lakh provides some offset for moderate LTCG amounts. Long-term equity investing remains tax-efficient.
